<!-- generated by setup-repository-workflow -->
# Agent artifacts

Agent workflows use two repository-local tiers:

- `docs/agents/runs/<workflow>/<run-id>/` — disposable working material such as prompts, manifests, logs, shards, and intermediate reviewer output. The directory is ignored by Git so a fixed-point review does not change the reviewed diff.
- `docs/agents/reports/<workflow>/<slug>.md` — an intentionally retained final synthesis, decision, or owner-facing report with a named consumer.

The workflow that creates a run owns its contents and cleanup. Remove a working run when its task or review is accepted, superseded, or abandoned. Do not promote raw corpora, credentials, private customer data, or generated logs into tracked documentation.

Chat output is sufficient unless a later session, owner, CI job, or decision explicitly consumes a persisted report. A retained report links its source revision or fixed point and states what it does not prove.

Every workflow resolves these roles through `docs/agents/artifact-paths.json`; individual skills do not hardcode a different root.
